pattern: Write tool called but not in tools list
description: Write tool invoked at line 294 to create temp-agent files but is absent from tools: [Read, Grep, Task] — agent will fail at runtime if tool list is enforced
suggested_fix: Add Write to the tools list: tools: [Read, Grep, Task, Write]

pattern: AskUserQuestion tool used but not declared in tools list
description: AskUserQuestion is the agent's primary interaction mechanism (used 15+ times) but is not declared in tools: [Read, Grep, Task]
suggested_fix: Add AskUserQuestion to the tools list: tools: [Read, Grep, Task, Write, AskUserQuestion]

pattern: Hardcoded absolute path with author username; referenced file missing from repo
description: Absolute path /Users/mbroler/.claude/plugins/repos/orchestration/docs/TEMP-SCRIPTS-DETECTION-GUIDE.md embeds author's macOS username and points to a file that does not exist anywhere in the repository
suggested_fix: Either create docs/TEMP-SCRIPTS-DETECTION-GUIDE.md and reference it via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}, or remove the READ THIS directive and inline the key detection rules

pattern: Referenced file does not exist in repo
description: References docs/TEMP-SCRIPTS-DETECTION-GUIDE.md which is absent from the repository — the link silently fails
suggested_fix: Create docs/TEMP-SCRIPTS-DETECTION-GUIDE.md or update the reference to point to an existing document covering temp-script detection

pattern: Five relative links point to missing examples/ sub-directory
description: Links to examples/sequential.md, examples/parallel.md, examples/conditional.md, examples/error-handling.md, and examples/checkpoints.md — the skills/executing-workflows/examples/ directory does not exist
suggested_fix: Create the skills/executing-workflows/examples/ directory with stub files for each referenced topic, or remove the broken links and point to docs/reference/examples.md instead

description: User-supplied workflow name from {{ARGS}} is used as-is in curl -o ./examples/<workflow-name>.flow — a name containing ../ segments could write files outside the examples/ directory
suggested_fix: Sanitize the filename: extract only the basename component and reject names containing path separators before constructing the -o argument

description: Skill instructs pip install and npm install patterns with variables sourced from workflow context; no version pinning requirement is shown, and installs run in the user's global environment
suggested_fix: Add a note requiring pinned versions in requirements.txt/package.json and recommend using virtual environments (python -m venv) or isolated node_modules rather than global installs

description: rm -rf ./temp-scripts/* and rm -f ./temp-agents/*.md executed as cleanup shell commands; limited blast radius and gated by user confirmation, but unconditional glob deletes are worth noting
suggested_fix: Add a CWD sanity check before rm to ensure the paths resolve within the expected working directory; already user-confirmation-gated which is good practice

description: commands/run.md has no name field in frontmatter; all 10 command files share this pattern suggesting intentional convention, but reduces AI discoverability
suggested_fix: Add name: run (or the canonical slash-command name) to the YAML frontmatter of each command file

description: commands/pull.md has no name field in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add name: pull to the YAML frontmatter

description: commands/init.md has no name field in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add name: init to the YAML frontmatter

description: commands/template.md has no name field in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add name: template to the YAML frontmatter

description: commands/examples.md has no name field in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add name: examples to the YAML frontmatter

description: commands/orchestrate.md has no name field in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add name: orchestrate to the YAML frontmatter

description: commands/help.md has no name field in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add name: help to the YAML frontmatter

description: commands/explain.md has no name field in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add name: explain to the YAML frontmatter

description: commands/menu.md has no name field in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add name: menu to the YAML frontmatter

description: commands/create.md has no name field in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add name: create to the YAML frontmatter

description: commands/run.md uses Task, AskUserQuestion, Bash tools but declares no allowed-tools field in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Task, AskUserQuestion, Bash] to frontmatter

description: commands/pull.md uses Bash (curl) but declares no allowed-tools field
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Bash] to frontmatter

description: commands/init.md uses Glob, Read, AskUserQuestion, Write but declares no allowed-tools
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Glob, Read, AskUserQuestion, Write] to frontmatter

description: commands/template.md uses Read, AskUserQuestion, Glob but declares no allowed-tools
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Read, AskUserQuestion, Glob] to frontmatter

description: commands/examples.md uses Read, Glob, AskUserQuestion but declares no allowed-tools
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Read, Glob, AskUserQuestion] to frontmatter

description: commands/orchestrate.md routes via SlashCommand but declares no allowed-tools
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [SlashCommand] to frontmatter

description: commands/help.md uses AskUserQuestion but declares no allowed-tools
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [AskUserQuestion] to frontmatter

description: commands/explain.md uses Read, AskUserQuestion but declares no allowed-tools
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Read, AskUserQuestion] to frontmatter

description: commands/menu.md uses AskUserQuestion, Glob, Grep but declares no allowed-tools
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [AskUserQuestion, Glob, Grep] to frontmatter

description: commands/create.md uses Task, AskUserQuestion but declares no allowed-tools
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Task, AskUserQuestion] to frontmatter

description: Agent does not declare a model field; model tier should be explicit for agents performing multi-step Socratic reasoning
suggested_fix: Add model: claude-sonnet-4-6 (or claude-opus-4-7 for higher reasoning demand) to the agent frontmatter

description: Agent does not declare a model field
suggested_fix: Add model: claude-sonnet-4-6 to the agent frontmatter

description: Output format is described inline across process steps rather than in a dedicated ## Output section; makes it hard for callers to parse expected return shape
suggested_fix: Add a dedicated ## Output section listing the final WorkflowRequirements JSON shape and the generated workflow syntax format

description: 'comprehensive prompt' uses the vague quantifier 'comprehensive' without defining what completeness criteria make a prompt comprehensive
suggested_fix: Replace with specific criteria: e.g., 'a prompt with role definition, input/output format, tool list, and 3+ examples'

description: 'Be thorough' is an unquantified instruction; thoroughness criteria are undefined
suggested_fix: Replace with specific guidance: e.g., 'Check all files matching *.js, *.ts, *.py for the listed vulnerability patterns'

description: 'Intuitive' used as a design principle without objective criteria for what makes a name intuitive
suggested_fix: Define a testable criterion: e.g., 'A new user should be able to guess the behavior from the name alone without reading docs'

description: 'Define behavior clearly' — 'clearly' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Document step-by-step what the element does, its preconditions, postconditions, and at least one example'

description: 'comprehensive documentation' — 'comprehensive' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'documentation including behavior, examples, and integration patterns'

description: 'Make prompts comprehensive and specific' — both 'comprehensive' and 'specific' are unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Include role, responsibilities (3+ items), output format, tool list, and at least one edge-case instruction'

description: 'Recommend appropriate tools' — 'appropriate' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'List the specific tools the agent will call (e.g., Read, Grep, Edit) and the condition that triggers each'

description: 'Manage errors gracefully' — 'gracefully' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'On agent failure, present retry/skip/abort options and preserve workflow state before halting'

description: 'Add review points for critical steps' — 'critical' is undefined
suggested_fix: Define critical: e.g., 'steps that write to production, delete data, or commit code'

description: 'Intuitive — Names/symbols hint at behavior' — no measurable criteria for intuitiveness
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'A user unfamiliar with the plugin should be able to infer the operator's effect from the symbol/name alone'

description: 'Only when truly needed' — 'truly needed' has no objective threshold
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Only create new syntax if the use case appears in at least two distinct workflows and existing syntax cannot express it'

description: 'complex multi-step tasks' — 'complex' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'tasks requiring external libraries, API authentication, or processing more than 20 items'

description: 'Set restrictive file permissions' — 'restrictive' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Set permissions to 700 (owner-only read/write/execute): chmod 700 /tmp/workflow-scripts/script.py'

description: 'complex multi-agent workflows' — 'complex' is unquantified in the skill trigger description
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'workflows requiring 3+ agents or conditional/parallel execution'

description: 'I'll proactively create temp scripts' — 'proactively' is vague without a defined detection threshold
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'I automatically create temp scripts when the user's request contains any of the trigger keywords listed below'

description: 'Show relevant syntax based on user's needs' — 'relevant' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Show the syntax example that matches the user's described operation type (sequential/parallel/conditional/retry)'

description: 'Show relevant example from docs/reference/examples.md' — 'relevant' is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Show the example whose workflow type (TDD/deploy/review) most closely matches the user's described process'

description: Command requires a workflow identifier argument but has no handler for when {{ARGS}} is empty; the Phase 1 check would silently proceed with an empty string
suggested_fix: Add an explicit empty-args guard at the top: if ARGS is empty, display usage instructions and list available registry entries

description: Template command has file-not-found handling but no explicit empty-args handler; user invoking /orchestration:template with no argument gets file search on empty string
suggested_fix: Add: if ARGS is empty, list all available templates from all locations and ask user to select one

description: Absolute path /Users/mbroler/.claude/... embeds author's macOS username; TEMP-SCRIPTS-DETECTION-GUIDE.md does not exist in the repo; same file referenced portably in creating-workflows/SKILL.md also resolves to nothing
suggested_fix: Create docs/TEMP-SCRIPTS-DETECTION-GUIDE.md and reference it as ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/docs/TEMP-SCRIPTS-DETECTION-GUIDE.md

description: Five category-specific example files referenced under skills/executing-workflows/examples/ but that directory does not exist in the repository
suggested_fix: Create skills/executing-workflows/examples/ with sequential.md, parallel.md, conditional.md, error-handling.md, and checkpoints.md stub files

description: Quick-reference panel lists /orchestration:run, /orchestration:template, /orchestration:examples, /orchestration:explain without indicating they are deprecated; users following help will invoke deprecated paths
suggested_fix: Annotate deprecated entries with (deprecated) or replace with the recommended skill-based alternatives in the help output

description: name: orchestration:executing-workflows uses the plugin namespace prefix; all other skills use bare names (e.g. name: managing-agents) — inconsistent naming convention across the skill set
suggested_fix: Standardize skill names: either add orchestration: prefix to all skills or remove it from executing-workflows and creating-workflows
